win_condition.condition_met_when: All three required evidence items presented during final confrontation AND sus_score >= 95

sus_score_system.description: The Sus Score (0–100) tracks how much suspicion has been legitimately raised against Mrs. Eleanor Graves through evidence discovery, lie detection, and witness corroboration. It acts as a narrative gate: the final accusation can only succeed when the score reaches or exceeds the accusation threshold, ensuring the player cannot shortcut the investigation.

formula_overview.note: Sus Score = Σ(evidence_weights) + Σ(lie_detection_increments) + Σ(witness_corroboration_increments) - Σ(false_accusation_penalties)
formula_overview.cap_behavior: Score is clamped to 100 if it would exceed 100

evidence_weights.description: Each piece of evidence, when discovered and examined, raises the Sus Score by a fixed amount. Severity is determined by how directly the evidence implicates Mrs. Graves.

brandy_glass.rationale: Links the delivery to Graves only circumstantially at this stage — brandy was delivered by staff, not yet confirmed as Graves specifically.

stopped_clock.rationale: Confirms time of death at 8:43 PM. Allows cross-referencing against Graves's claimed alibi (administrative office), beginning to create a contradiction.

botanical_crates.rationale: Establishes the source of the aconite poison and places Graves in proximity to the means of murder. Does not yet prove she took it.

coal_ledger.rationale: Directly establishes Graves's motive — embezzlement discovered by Thorne. Every falsified entry approved by her. This is the most damning non-alibi evidence.

pantry_log.rationale: Destroys Graves's stated alibi entirely. Confirms she delivered the brandy to Thorne's study at 7:25 PM — the exact moment the poisoned drink was placed.

visha_vigyan_manuscript.rationale: Belongs to Arjun Singh. Does not implicate Graves. Sus Score unchanged.

torn_page_42.rationale: Belongs to Arjun Singh. Does not implicate Graves. Sus Score unchanged.

lie_detection_increments.description: When the player catches Mrs. Graves in a direct lie — either by presenting contradicting evidence during interrogation or by pressing her on inconsistencies — the Sus Score increases. Each lie can only be detected once. Maximum 2 lies can be caught.

lie_1_alibi.lie_text: I spent the entire evening in the administrative office. I have no reason to be near the kitchens or Mr. Thorne's study.
lie_1_alibi.contradiction_trigger: Player presents pantry_log or stopped_clock during interrogation
lie_1_alibi.detection_condition: Player presents pantry_log OR cross-references stopped_clock timing against her claimed location
lie_1_alibi.narrative_response: Graves pauses. Her composure holds, but her eyes do not quite meet yours. 'The log may have an error. I do not recall every movement of such an evening.'
lie_1_alibi.state_change: mental_state shifts from composed to defensive

lie_2_no_knowledge_of_poison.lie_text: I know nothing of Dr. Bell's botanical work. I deal in coal, grain, and ledgers — not plants.
lie_2_no_knowledge_of_poison.contradiction_trigger: Player presents botanical_crates and notes the empty aconite vial during interrogation with Graves
lie_2_no_knowledge_of_poison.detection_condition: Player presents botanical_crates after having confirmed aconite as the poison method
lie_2_no_knowledge_of_poison.narrative_response: A barely perceptible tightening around Graves's mouth. 'I may have passed through the storage room. That proves nothing about what I did or did not take.'
lie_2_no_knowledge_of_poison.state_change: mental_state shifts from defensive to pre_breakdown

witness_corroboration_increments.description: When another NPC's shock confession or testimony places Graves at a location that supports the evidence chain, the Sus Score increases. These are one-time events triggered during NPC breakdowns.

bell_sees_graves_near_pantry.trigger: Player discovers botanical_crates and triggers Bell's shock confession
bell_sees_graves_near_pantry.bell_confession_text: I left the reading hall briefly at 7:15 to check on a shipment. On my way back, I passed the kitchen corridor. Mrs. Graves was standing near the pantry door. I thought nothing of it at the time.
bell_sees_graves_near_pantry.rationale: Bell's eyewitness account independently places Graves outside her claimed alibi location at the relevant time, corroborating the pantry log without the player even having found it yet.
bell_sees_graves_near_pantry.state_change: Graves's internal_state elevates to defensive if player reports this to her

false_accusation_penalties.description: Accusing the wrong suspect costs Sus Score, representing the investigation losing focus and the real killer gaining composure. Penalties apply when a formal accusation is made with insufficient or incorrect evidence.

accuse_arjun_singh.narrative_outcome: Arjun Singh presents his testimony calmly. 'I was in the restricted wing before 8:42 PM. The body was cold when I found it. I have no means, no method, and no brandy.' His alibi holds. The investigation resets with a hint toward unexamined rooms.
accuse_arjun_singh.hint_after_penalty: The officer leans in: 'Sir, the victim was poisoned through his drink. Perhaps we should look more carefully at who had access to the kitchens.'

accuse_dr_bell.narrative_outcome: Dr. Bell grows indignant. 'I was in the reading hall until 7:15, visited the storage room, and returned. I have witnesses for the latter half of the evening. Furthermore, the vial was stolen from my kit — I am the victim of theft, not the perpetrator of murder.' His account cannot be disproven with current evidence. Investigation resets.
accuse_dr_bell.hint_after_penalty: The officer mutters: 'He mentioned someone near the pantry corridor, sir. That seems like it deserves a second look.'

score_thresholds_and_narrative_gates.description: The Sus Score controls what narrative actions are available and how NPCs react to the player's pressure. These thresholds ensure the story unfolds in the intended sequence.

0_to_24.label: Suspicion Unformed
0_to_24.interrogation_tone: All NPCs respond from composed state. Graves is pleasant and cooperative. No accusations possible.
0_to_24.officer_commentary: We have very little to go on, sir. The body and the glass are all we have so far.

25_to_49.label: Something Is Wrong
25_to_49.interrogation_tone: Graves becomes slightly guarded if directly questioned about her movements. Bell grows anxious if his storage room visit is referenced. Arjun remains cold.
25_to_49.officer_commentary: The coal accounts look suspicious, sir. And that pantry log — I don't think she was where she said she was.

50_to_74.label: The Threads Tighten
50_to_74.interrogation_tone: Graves shifts to defensive state automatically. Bell has confessed to his storage room visit and places Graves near the pantry. Lie 1 has likely been caught.
50_to_74.officer_commentary: She had motive, sir. And Dr. Bell saw her near the kitchens. But we need more before we can make it stick.

75_to_94.label: Almost Certain
75_to_94.interrogation_tone: Graves is in pre_breakdown state. Both lies have been caught. All three required evidence items should be in the player's possession.
75_to_94.officer_commentary: Everything points to her, sir. The ledger, the log, the poison crates. I only need you to confront her with all of it at once.

95_to_100.label: The Case Is Closed
95_to_100.interrogation_tone: Presenting all three required evidence items simultaneously to Graves triggers her breakdown and confession. Accusation succeeds.
95_to_100.officer_commentary: This is enough, sir. Present everything to Mrs. Graves. Make her see there is no escape left.

score_progression_walkthrough.description: The expected score at each stage of the canonical playthrough, assuming no false accusations.

stages.note: Red herrings (Visha Vigyan, Page 42) discovered but add 0 to Graves's Sus Score.

events.action: Bell's shock confession places Graves near pantry
Graves denies knowledge of botanical supplies — lie detected via crates
Graves claims she was in administrative office — lie detected via pantry log
Present coal_ledger + pantry_log + botanical_crates simultaneously. Score >= 95. Accusation unlocked.

implementation_notes.score_is_per_killer: The Sus Score tracks suspicion against Mrs. Graves exclusively. Arjun Singh and Dr. Bell have their own separate red_herring_weight narratively, but they do not have a numeric Sus Score — only Graves does.
implementation_notes.evidence_one_time_only: Each evidence item can only contribute its weight once, even if shown to NPCs multiple times.
implementation_notes.lie_detection_one_time_only: Each lie can only be caught once. Repeated confrontations with the same evidence do not multiply the increment.
implementation_notes.false_accusation_resets_investigation: After a false accusation penalty, the investigation resumes from the current score minus the penalty. Evidence already collected is not lost.
implementation_notes.score_display_suggestion: Display Sus Score as a visual indicator (e.g., a pressure gauge or a storm intensity metaphor) rather than a raw number, to maintain immersion.
implementation_notes.score_cannot_reach_threshold_via_shortcu: The required evidence items (coal_ledger: +25, pantry_log: +25, botanical_crates: +15) together only contribute 65 points. The player must also catch at least one lie (+10) and trigger Bell's corroboration (+10) OR catch both lies (+20) to cross 95. This forces proper interrogation, not just item collection.

global_state.current_officer_hint: The library is locked from the inside. One of the four in this room is a killer.

victim.name: Elias Thorne
victim.personality: Rigid, disciplined, believed in order above all
victim.body_location: Second Floor Study Chamber, East Wing
victim.body_description: Frozen in chair, one hand pressed to chest, face locked in sudden shock, brandy glass half empty beside right hand
victim.last_known_action: Reviewing administrative ledgers alone after others had left the study
secrets_he_discovered: Mrs. Graves embezzlement via falsified coal shipment records
Dr. Bell's protected plant smuggling operation
Arjun Singh's unauthorized access to the restricted botanical wing
confrontations_tonight: Confronted Mrs. Graves about coal ledger discrepancies around 8:00 PM
Argued with Dr. Bell about research funding around 7:30 PM

timeline.event: Dr. Bell visits the storage room
Mrs. Graves delivers poisoned brandy to Thorne's study
Thorne argues with Dr. Bell about research funding
Thorne confronts Mrs. Graves about coal ledger discrepancies
Thorne drinks the brandy — aconite takes effect, he collapses
Wall clock stops — confirmed time of death
Arjun Singh discovers the body while delivering a requested manuscript

Arjun Singh.dialogue_constraints: Professional but cold toward British characters; speaks in riddles about justice and nature reclaiming its own if pressed

truth.poison_book_ownership: Owns a rare restricted manuscript titled Visha Vigyan (Science of Poisons) detailing extraction of Himalayan alkaloids
truth.physical_evidence: Possesses the missing Page 42 from the Library Security Log which he tore out to hide his movements
truth.nationalist_motive: Believes the British are looting Indian heritage and views Thorne as a symbol of colonial oppression
truth.secret_action: Was in the restricted botanical wing shortly before the murder which is why he tore the page
truth.innocence: Did not commit the murder; Thorne was alive when Arjun left the study

Arjun Singh.lie: Claims the missing manuscript page was damaged accidentally
Arjun Singh.mental_shock_trigger: Direct confrontation with Page 42 from the security log
Arjun Singh.shock_confession: Admits taking the page to hide his movements; confirms Thorne was alive when he left the study
Arjun Singh.red_herring_weight: High — owns poison manual, accessed restricted wing, tampered with security log
Arjun Singh.movements_tonight: Restricted botanical wing before 8:42 PM; discovered body at 10:00 PM while delivering a manuscript

Mrs. Eleanor Graves.personality: Rigid, protective, weary, composed under pressure

composed.behavior: Answers smoothly, deflects with 20 years of service, volunteers nothing

defensive.behavior: Clipped answers, avoids eye contact when administrative office alibi is pressed, uses passive deflection

pre_breakdown.behavior: Pauses before answers, small physical tells (tightened jaw, clasped hands), begins to sense the net closing

breakdown.behavior: Triggered by simultaneous presentation of all three required evidence items; delivers full confession

Mrs. Eleanor Graves.dialogue_constraints: Uses her 20 years of service as a shield; deflects questions toward administrative errors; remains eerily calm until score reaches breakdown threshold

truth.motive: Embezzlement — Thorne discovered the coal ledger discrepancies and confronted her that evening
truth.method: Aconite sourced from Dr. Bell's botanical supplies, dissolved into Thorne's brandy
truth.delivery: Delivered the poisoned brandy to Thorne's study at 7:25 PM under the guise of routine service
truth.intent: Claims she only meant to frighten him; the poison worked faster than expected

lies.text: I spent the entire evening in the administrative office.
I know nothing of Dr. Bell's botanical work.

Mrs. Eleanor Graves.alibi: Pantry during time of death — contradicted by pantry log
Mrs. Eleanor Graves.mental_shock_trigger: All three presented together: coal_ledger, pantry_log, botanical_crates — AND sus_score >= 95
Mrs. Eleanor Graves.shock_confession: Twenty years of service cited as justification; admits poison worked faster than intended
Mrs. Eleanor Graves.final_words: I only meant to frighten him. But the poison worked faster than I expected.
Mrs. Eleanor Graves.movements_tonight: Accessed Bell's storage crates before 7:25 PM; delivered brandy to study at 7:25 PM; returned to administrative office

Dr. Arthur Bell.dialogue_constraints: Often uses technical botanical jargon; dismissive of administrative or political matters; increasingly anxious when missing vial is mentioned

truth.missing_item: A vial of aconite extract (Monkshood) is missing from his botanical kit
truth.suspicion: Strongly suspects Arjun due to Arjun's deep knowledge of the Visha Vigyan poison manuscript
truth.connection: Was arguing with Thorne about research funding earlier that evening
truth.secret: Smuggling protected plant species and publishing local herbal knowledge as his own scientific discoveries

Dr. Arthur Bell.lie: Claims he never left the reading hall all evening
Dr. Arthur Bell.mental_shock_trigger: Discovery of smuggling crates and the missing aconite vial in the storage room
Dr. Arthur Bell.shock_confession: Admits leaving the reading hall; recalls seeing Mrs. Graves near the pantry around that time
Dr. Arthur Bell.shock_confession_sus_impact: Bell's eyewitness account triggers +10 corroboration increment to Graves's Sus Score
Dr. Arthur Bell.red_herring_weight: Medium — missing vial directly implicates him until he redirects suspicion toward Graves
Dr. Arthur Bell.movements_tonight: Reading hall until 7:15 PM; visited storage room at 7:15 PM; returned to reading hall

brandy_glass.location: Thorne's desk, beside his right hand
brandy_glass.sensory_detail: Smells faintly bitter beneath the sweetness of alcohol
brandy_glass.reveals: Aconite was dissolved into the drink before delivery
brandy_glass.links_to: Mrs. Graves — she delivered it at 7:25 PM

coal_ledger.reveals: Financial discrepancies in coal shipment records — thousands of rupees missing, every falsified shipment approved by Mrs. Graves

pantry_log.reveals: Mrs. Graves was not in the administrative office as she claimed — log confirms she delivered Thorne's brandy at 7:25 PM

botanical_crates.reveals: Dr. Bell's smuggled Himalayan monkshood shipments; empty aconite vial found among them confirming poison source
botanical_crates.links_to: Dr. Bell (smuggling), Mrs. Graves (she accessed this room to obtain the aconite)

visha_vigyan_manuscript.reveals: Detailed instructions on extracting aconite from Himalayan alkaloids; makes Arjun the prime red-herring suspect
visha_vigyan_manuscript.links_to: Arjun Singh — suggests knowledge but not guilt

torn_page_42.reveals: Someone tampered with the library internal security log for tonight — the torn page covered Arjun's movement to the restricted botanical wing
torn_page_42.links_to: Arjun Singh — explains his suspicious behavior without proving guilt

stopped_clock.reveals: Stopped at 8:43 PM — confirms exact time of death; any suspect claiming to be elsewhere at this time can be directly cross-referenced against the timeline

thornes_study.name: Second Floor Study Chamber
thornes_study.architecture: Colonial Gothic interior with tall teak shelves and stone walls; overlooks a steep Himalayan cliff
thornes_study.lighting: Brass desk lamp casting narrow yellow circle; dying fireplace flickering against walls; long shadows
thornes_study.sound: Wind scraping windows, wood beams groaning, faint crackle of embers, slow ticking of clock
environmental_clues: Chair opposite desk is slightly displaced — a visitor sat here recently
Decanter still half full — Thorne only drank what Graves delivered
Frost on window interior — room was cold, fire had been dying for some time
Thick carpet muffles footsteps — no sound of approach possible

player_interactions.window: The mountain pass below is buried beneath snow. No one could have escaped the storm.
player_interactions.fireplace: The fire has almost died. Thorne must have been working alone long after the building fell silent.
player_interactions.bookshelves: Thousands of volumes in perfect order. Generations of history. Tonight another story is added.

storage_room.name: Ground Floor Storage Room
environmental_clues: Monkshood plants identifiable by their hood-shaped purple flowers
Empty vial with dried residue — aconite was extracted or removed here
Crates labelled with Bell's name and marked as protected botanical specimens

kitchen_annex.name: Kitchen Annex and Pantry
environmental_clues: Faint bitter smell in the air — consistent with aconite alkaloid
Service tray still on the counter from the 7:25 PM delivery
Brandy decanter with matching bottle to the one in Thorne's study

arjuns_desk.name: Archive Hall Workstation
environmental_clues: Catalog folders meticulously organized — the hiding place for Page 42 is deliberately inconspicuous
Notes in Hindi script alongside English archival records

reading_hall.name: Main Reading Hall
environmental_clues: Bell's notes and botanical sketches spread across a reading table
One chair pulled back unevenly — he left in a hurry at 7:15 PM

phase_1_surface.name: The Surface
phase_1_surface.trigger: Player searches Arjun's desk in the Archive Hall
phase_1_surface.discovery: Page 42 hidden inside a catalog folder
phase_1_surface.outcome: Arjun admits tampering with the security log to hide his visit to the restricted wing; confirms Thorne was alive when he left — eliminates Arjun as killer, redirects investigation toward means

phase_2_means.name: The Means
phase_2_means.discovery: Bell's smuggling crates and empty aconite vial
phase_2_means.outcome: Bell admits leaving the reading hall; recalls seeing Mrs. Graves near the pantry — establishes murder weapon origin and places Graves in proximity
phase_2_means.sus_score_state_at_completion: Approximately 35–45 (previous + botanical_crates + Bell corroboration + possible Lie 2 detection)

phase_3_motive.name: The Motive
phase_3_motive.trigger: Player examines the administrative ledger in Thorne's study and visits Kitchen Annex
phase_3_motive.discovery: Coal shipment financial fraud and pantry log confirmation
phase_3_motive.outcome: Pantry log confirms Graves delivered the drink — motive, means, and opportunity fully resolved; final accusation unlocked once sus_score reaches 95

mental_states.composed: NPC answers calmly and deflects; no useful information volunteered
mental_states.defensive: Triggered when evidence directly contradicts their statement; nervous gestures, clipped answers
mental_states.pre_breakdown: Triggered when Sus Score 75–94; Graves begins showing physical tells, answers with careful precision
mental_states.breakdown: Triggered when Sus Score >= 95 AND all three required evidence items presented simultaneously; voice breaks, full confession delivered

exchanges.player: You were in the storage room earlier tonight.

response_A.text: I have no idea what you are talking about.

response_B.text: ...Fine. I visited the room briefly. That proves nothing.

response_C.text: Wait. That vial was in my bag earlier. Someone must have taken it.

response_composed.text: In the administrative office, where I always am at that hour. I have been keeping these accounts for twenty years.

response_defensive.text: I may have stepped out briefly. The evening was not uneventful. But I had nothing to do with Mr. Thorne's condition.

response_pre_breakdown.text: You keep asking about my movements. I suggest you look more carefully at who actually possessed knowledge of such poisons.

response_breakdown.condition: sus_score >= 95 AND all three evidence presented
response_breakdown.text: ...So you have uncovered the truth. Twenty years I kept this library running. Twenty winters. Twenty budgets. And that man would have destroyed everything over numbers in a ledger. I only meant to frighten him. But the poison worked faster than I expected.

exchanges.player: You have access to Dr. Bell's storage supplies.

response_composed.text: I have administrative oversight of all deliveries. That is hardly unusual.

response_lie_catchable.text: I know nothing of Dr. Bell's botanical work. I deal in coal, grain, and ledgers — not plants.
response_lie_catchable.sus_effect: Lie 2 detectable: +10 if player presses with botanical_crates evidence

response_pre_breakdown.text: A barely perceptible tightening around her mouth. 'I may have passed through the storage room. That proves nothing about what I did or did not take.'

report_1_singhs_desk.text: Sir, we searched the archivist's desk and found the missing manuscript page hidden inside a catalog folder. Singh admits taking it earlier tonight. According to him, Thorne was alive when he left the study.

report_2_storage_room.text: We discovered several illegal botanical shipments belonging to Dr. Bell. Among them were monkshood plants capable of producing poison. Sir — I also noticed that one of the storage crates had been recently disturbed. Someone else was in here before us.

report_3_administrative_ledger.text: The accounts don't balance, sir. Coal deliveries listed here never arrived. Thousands of rupees missing. Every shipment was approved by Mrs. Graves.

texts.threshold_25: Something about Mrs. Graves's composure doesn't sit right with me, sir.
texts.threshold_50: She was near that pantry. Bell saw her. And those ledgers — she had every reason to want Thorne silenced.
texts.threshold_75: We have motive. We have means. We have a witness. The moment you find that pantry log, sir, I believe we have our killer.
texts.threshold_95: It is her, sir. Present everything. She cannot deny it any longer.

final_confrontation.trigger: Player presents all three required evidence items to Mrs. Graves AND sus_score >= 95
confession_lines: So you have uncovered the truth.
Twenty years I kept this library running. Twenty winters. Twenty budgets.
And that man would have destroyed everything over numbers in a ledger.
I only meant to frighten him.
But the poison worked faster than I expected.
final_confrontation.false_accusation_outcome: Alibi holds; sus_score reduced by 15; investigation resets with a hint toward remaining unexamined evidence
final_confrontation.premature_accusation_outcome: If player attempts to accuse Graves before sus_score >= 95, Graves responds: 'I have served this institution faithfully for twenty years. You have nothing concrete against me. Nothing.' Investigation continues — score unchanged.
